key_insight: MLIP Studio unifies 60+ universal machine learning interatomic potentials into one interactive platform for benchmarking, simulation, and model selection across molecules and materials.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the barrier to using foundation ML potentials in practical computational chemistry and materials workflows by enabling faster pre-optimization, automated evaluation, and easier comparison against DFT.
method: Interactive web platform for property prediction, geometry optimization, vibrational analysis, equation-of-state studies, spin-state determination, and custom model deployment.
Automated benchmarking tools with parity plots, sortable error tables, and element-wise outlier detection.
Supports high-throughput comparison of MLIPs against reference data and computational performance metrics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that MLIP-based pre-optimization can reduce subsequent DFT optimization effort by about 33×, and includes a case study on CrCl3 on sapphire for cross-model comparison.
watch_item: Model rankings and transferability can be task- and system-dependent, so users still need careful validation before relying on a chosen MLIP for new chemistry or materials.
paper_title: MLIP Studio: An Open Platform for Interactive Benchmarking and Atomistic Simulations Using Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
